product_overview: Retro Tool is a real-time web-based retrospective application (Mad/Sad/Glad) that lets teams create short-lived rooms, collect draft/published entries, run timed retros, rate entries, and export selected results. It uses session-based auth, in-memory storage, and Socket.IO for live collaboration.
core_goals: Provide a lightweight, zero-install retrospective room experience with 6-digit room codes and an invite link.
Enable contributors to create draft entries, publish them, and collaboratively select/export the most important items.
Support a creator-driven timer lifecycle (start, extend, reopen, terminate) with server-side enforcement and synchronized clients.
Allow post-retro ratings of published entries (excluding self-ratings) and export selected published entries to Excel.
Keep the app real-time via Socket.IO for entry, participant, and timer updates while remaining stateless/persistent-free (in-memory).

features.name: Room Management
features.description: Create, join, list and retrieve rooms using 6-digit codes; enforce participant and time limits; auto-generate invite links and clean up empty/expired rooms.
user_flows: POST /api/create-room with {name, participantLimit?, timeLimit?} -> Receive 201 with {code, inviteLink} -> GET /api/room/:code with session -> Receive 200 with room data (creator auto-joined)
POST /api/join-room with {code, username} -> Receive 200 and session cookie -> GET /api/room/:code -> Receive 200 with room data and participants list
POST /api/join-room with {code (nonexistent), username} -> Receive 404 with error (room not found)
POST /api/create-room with {name, participantLimit: 100} (invalid limit >50) -> Receive 400 validation error

features.name: Entry Management (Mad / Sad / Glad)
features.description: Create draft entries in three categories, edit/delete drafts, publish entries when ready, and enforce content length and ownership rules.
user_flows: POST /api/room/:code/entry with {category: 'mad'|'sad'|'glad', text: '...' } -> Receive 201 with entry {id, draft: true, published: false} -> POST /api/room/:code/entry/:id/publish -> Receive 200 with entry {published: true} -> GET /api/room/:code -> Receive 200 where published entry is visible to others
POST /api/room/:code/entry with {category, text} -> Receive 201 -> PUT /api/room/:code/entry/:id with {text: 'edited text'} -> Receive 200 (edit allowed because draft)
POST /api/room/:code/entry with {category, text: '<501 chars>' } -> Receive 400 validation error (max 500 chars)
PUT /api/room/:code/entry/:id with {text} for an already published entry -> Receive 403 (only draft entries editable by owner)

features.name: Timer & Room Lifecycle
features.description: Creator controls the retrospective lifecycle: start the timer, extend remaining time, reopen the room (remove time limit), or terminate the retro early. Server enforces timer state and automatic expiration.
user_flows: POST /api/room/:code/start (creator) -> Receive 200 with timerStarted -> GET /api/room/:code/timer -> Receive 200 with {started: true, startedAt, expiresAt}
POST /api/room/:code/extend-time with {minutes: 10} (creator) -> Receive 200 with timeExtended -> GET /api/room/:code/timer -> Receive 200 with updated expiresAt
POST /api/room/:code/reopen (creator) -> Receive 200 with reopened: true -> GET /api/room/:code/timer -> Receive 200 indicating no active time limit
POST /api/room/:code/terminate (creator) -> Receive 200 with terminated: true -> GET /api/room/:code -> Receive 200 showing retro ended
POST /api/room/:code/start (non-creator) -> Receive 403 forbidden (only creator may start timer)
POST /api/room/:code/extend-time with {minutes: 120} (invalid >60) -> Receive 400 validation error

features.name: Rating System
features.description: After the retro ends (time expired or terminated), participants can rate published entries 1-5 stars. Users cannot rate their own entries and averages are computed excluding the owner.
user_flows: POST /api/room/:code/entry/:id/rate with {rating: 4} after retro ended -> Receive 200 with {entryId, averageRating, ratingsCount}
POST /api/room/:code/entry/:id/rate with {rating: 6} -> Receive 400 validation error (rating must be 1-5)
POST /api/room/:code/entry/:id/rate by the entry owner -> Receive 400 (cannot rate own entry)
POST /api/room/:code/entry/:id/rate before retro has ended -> Receive 400 (rating not allowed until retro ends)

features.name: Export (Excel)
features.description: Creator can export selected AND published entries to an Excel (.xlsx) file containing Category, Entry text, and Username columns.
user_flows: GET /api/room/:code/export (creator) -> Receive 200 with Excel (.xlsx) file attachment containing selected & published entries
GET /api/room/:code/export (non-creator) -> Receive 403 forbidden (only creator can export)
GET /api/room/:code/export (creator) when no selected+published entries exist -> Receive 400 with message (nothing to export)

features.name: Real-Time Collaboration (Socket.IO)
features.description: Clients connect over Socket.IO for live roomState, newEntry, participant updates, timer events, and selection/publish broadcasts. Server shares sessions with sockets to enforce permissions.
user_flows: Socket.IO connect -> Emit 'joinRoom' with {code} -> Receive 'roomState' event with current room and participant list -> POST /api/room/:code/entry with {category, text} -> Receive 201 -> Receive 'newEntry' socket event broadcast to room with entry payload
Socket.IO emit 'joinRoom' with {code (invalid)} -> Receive 'error' socket event with message (room not found)
Socket.IO connection existing participant disconnects -> Server broadcasts 'participantUpdate' to room -> Remaining clients receive updated participant list
POST /api/room/:code/toggle-entry with {entryId} (creator) -> Receive 200 -> Server emits 'entryToggled' to room -> Clients receive updated entry.selected state

features.name: Participant & Selection Management
features.description: Creator can select/unselect entries and view participants; participants can be listed via API. Room cleanup removes empty rooms after configured interval.
user_flows: GET /api/room/:code/participants -> Receive 200 with participants array
POST /api/room/:code/toggle-entry with {entryId} (creator) -> Receive 200 with entry selection state -> GET /api/room/:code -> Receive 200 reflecting selection change
POST /api/room/:code/toggle-entry (non-creator) -> Receive 403 forbidden (only creator may toggle selection)
After all participants leave, room is auto-deleted by cleanup task (no API interaction) -> Subsequent GET /api/room/:code -> Receive 404 (room not found)

features.name: Internationalization & Client Localization
features.description: Frontend auto-detects browser language and loads one of the supported language packs (TR, EN, DE, ES); choice persists in localStorage for future visits.
user_flows: GET / -> Receive 200 index.html -> Client requests public/languages/{lang}.js based on navigator.language -> Receive 200 language JS and UI renders localized strings
GET / -> Receive 200 index.html with no supported language detected -> Client loads default public/languages/tr.js -> UI displays Turkish by default
Client selects language in UI -> Client saves language code to localStorage -> Next GET / returns UI using persisted language (client side behavior)

project.name: retro-tool
project.description: Real-time web-based retrospective tool for agile teams using Mad/Sad/Glad format with WebSocket support

responsibilities: In-memory room and user session management

shared.description: Shared utilities, validation, i18n, API helpers

rating: Only after retro ends (time expired or terminated)

fields: entries: {mad: Entry[], sad: Entry[], glad: Entry[]}
